data.title: Zimmer Biomet falls after CEO exit, unchanged growth outlook
data.description:
Zimmer Biomet (ZBH) lost ~6%, potentially on track to record its biggest single-day decline since November on Tuesday after the orthopedic device maker announced the departure of its CFO and maintained its full-year revenue outlook with its Q1 2026 results.

Suketu Upadhyay, who served Zimmer (ZBH) as CFO since 2019, will depart effective April 28 for a new career opportunity, with Chief Accounting Officer Paul Stellato taking over on an interim basis until a permanent replacement is found, the company said.

Stellato, who joined Zimmer (ZBH) in 2022, has helped the company implement its capital allocation strategy, including its recent acquisitions and share repurchase program, the Warsaw, Indiana-based MedTech noted.

"We are deeply grateful for Suky's leadership and contributions to Zimmer Biomet over the past seven years," added CEO Ivan Tornos.

As for Q1 financials, Zimmer (ZBH) reported $2.09 of adjusted diluted earnings per share on $2.1B in sales with ~16% YoY and ~9% YoY growth, exceeding the consensus by $20M and $0.23, respectively.

ZBH’s Hips net sales for the quarter reached $524M with a ~6% YoY growth, beating the consensus of $517.7M according to Bloomberg data, while Knees net sales jumped ~5% YoY to $828.6M, missing $832.3M projected by analysts.

Looking ahead, the company estimates 2.5% YoY - 4.5% YoY revenue growth for 2026, unchanged from its prior forecast and ~3.9% YoY growth in the consensus. However, Zimmer (ZBH) lifted its adjusted EPS outlook to $8.40 - $8.55 from $8.30 - $8.45 in the previous guidance, in line with the average analyst estimate of $8.40.

data.title: Resorts World becomes New York City's first full casino as Wynn, MGM, and Caesars sit out the party
data.description:
Resorts World New York City held a ribbon-cutting ceremony and took in the first official table games bets as part of a phase one opening of its new casino in New York City. The $5B expansion project includes adding a hotel wing, a 7,000-seat entertainment venue, an indoor dayclub with pools, a spa, and much more at the former racino site in Queens near Aqueduct Racetrack.

The casino is operated by the Genting Group, a global company founded in 1965, with destination resort operations in the Catskills, Hudson Valley, Las Vegas, Bimini, Malaysia, Singapore, and the United Kingdom. 

Resorts World's Queens casino is not the only full-scale casino coming to New York City soon. In December, the New York State Gaming Commission approved three locations, including a Bally's (BALY) location on land next to the former Trump Golf Links at Ferry Point in the Bronx and a casino and entertainment center near Citi Field, backed by Mets owner Steve Cohen. Both are ground-up construction projects, with anticipated opening timelines pushed out to 2030, which gives Resorts World New York City a multi-year window as the only full casino game in town.

Wynn Resorts (WYNN) and MGM Resorts (MGM) both backed out of the New York City casino license bidding process, while Caesars Entertainment's (CZR) Times Square casino project in New York City was voted down in September of last year and did not advance to the state licensing stage.

data.title: Ecolab slips after in-line Q1; Q2 earnings guidance mostly below consensus
data.description:
Ecolab (ECL) down 2.4% in early trading Tuesday after reporting Q1 adjusted earnings that matched Wall Street estimates and issuing earnings guidance for Q2 of $2.02-$2.12/share, compared to the $2.10 FactSet consensus estimate.

Q1 profit rose to $432.6M, or $1.52/share, from $402.5M, or $1.41/share, in the year-earlier quarter, while revenues rose 10% Y/Y to $4.07B, driven by strong value pricing, volume growth, and improved productivity, the company said.

Q1 sales in the life sciences business increased 11% Y/Y, driven by bioprocessing, which more than doubled its sales during the quarter, while post-elimination revenue rose 7%, and sales at Ecolab's (ECL) digital and global high-tech units both jumped more than 20%, the company said.

Ecolab (ECL) said it added a global energy surcharge during the quarter to mitigate the rising cost of fuel, as it expects commodity costs to increase by a high-single-digit percentage starting in Q2, with fuel prices anticipated to stay elevated.

The company said it expects the surcharge will offset costs and its margin will stabilize once it reaches this year's H2, leading it to maintain its full-year earnings guidance of $8.43-$8.63/share, above the $8.42 FactSet consensus.

data.title: BridgeBio seesaws after settlement in Pfizer Vyndamax patent dispute (update)
data.description:
Update 10:35: Adds Pfizer statement.

BridgeBio (BBIO) fell 1.3% after originally gaining 11% as a court filing disclosed a settlement in a generic patent case over Pfizer's (PFE) Vyndamax drug. Shares of Pfizer ticked lower by 0.7%.

Pfizer (PFE) and generic drug maker Hikma agreed to stipulate and agreed that Pfizer’s action against Hikma is hereby dismissed without prejudice, according to a court filing  on Tuesday.

The settlement with Hikma comes after Pfizer (PFE)  agreed to settle last week with two other generic drugmakers, Dexcel Pharma and Cipla, it had sued over a patent dispute over its Vyndamax drug. The settlements come as a patent trial was slated to start this week.

These settlements extend the effective U.S. patent expiry date for Vyndamax to June 1, 2031, subject to the outcome of other litigation, Pfizer said in a statement on Tuesday.  Pfizer had previously anticipated a "significant decline" in U.S. revenue for Vyndamax beginning in 2029 upon patent expiry. 

"We are very pleased by this outcome, both for patients and in recognition of the value of our innovative science and the strength of our patents," said Aamir Malik, Executive Vice President and Chief U.S. Commercial Officer, Pfizer, in the statement.

Barclays analyst Eliana Merle on Friday said she sees base case settlement outcome could be a generic Vyndamax entry in 2031. BridgeBio (BBIO) makes a drug similar to Vyndamax/tafamidis called Attruby for a deadly form of heart disease.

"We think BBIO shares could be up ~30-40% (PFE settles/wins scenario) vs down ~20-30% (PFE loses scenario); though we think regardless of outcome this could still be a clearing event and shares could recover from there," Merle, who has an overweight rating and $157 price target on BBIO, wrote in a note on Friday.

data.title: AbbVie says Rinvoq bests Humira in head-to-head study in rheumatoid arthritis
data.description:
• AbbVie (ABBV) said that Rinvoq (upadacitinib) had better efficacy compared to Humira (adalimumab) in a head-to-head study that enrolled rheumatoid arthritis patients who failed on a TNF inhibitor.
  • Results from the phase 3b/4 SELECT-SWITCH trial of around 500 individuals found 43.3% of patients on Rinvoq at week 12 saw low disease activity (measured by DAS28-CRP≤3.2)  compared to 22.4% of those on Humira, meeting the primary endpoint.
  • Also, 28.4% of individuals receiving Rinvoq achieved another low disease measure, DAS28-CRP<2.6, compared to 14.5% of those on Humira. This was a secondary endpoint.
  • Rinvoq is a JAK inhibitor, while Humira is a TNF inhibitor. Humira is also facing biosimilar competition.

data.title: Teladoc Q1 Preview: Profits seen improving, BetterHelp operations in focus
data.description:
Teladoc Health’s (TDOC) Q1 loss is projected to see a 26% improvement in its earnings on April 27, after market close, with continued improvement expected from its BetterHelp segment.

The consensus EPS Estimate is -$0.39, while revenue is seen falling 3% to $610.59M. Ahead of the earnings, sentiment appears cautious as analysts have made no upward revisions for earnings or revenue. Meanwhile, they have issued 2 and 19 downward revisions, respectively.

BetterHelp's revenue declined 9% in 2025, in line with its last four years’ performance, showing flat or declining revenue, high cost of user acquisition, high rates of seasonal churn, and lack of recurring revenue. 

However, the business has shown improvement following its acquisition of UpLift in April 2025.

“To date, early results validate a change in the model. In 2025, BetterHelp generated about $13 million in insurance revenue, with management expecting this to grow to $75–$90 million in 2026, representing a 6-7x increase from the previous year,” Seeking Alpha analyst  Tyler Wiedwald noted.

The management sees BetterHelp 2026 revenue falling between 7% and 0.5% (vs. 2025), with insurance revenue expected at $75 million to $90 million and an exit annualized revenue run rate above $100 million. Direct-to-consumer revenue is projected to be down 14% to 9%, while international revenue is expected to see double-digit growth.

Some optimism is also centered around PulseAI under Teledoc’s integrated care segment, which has often had the highest number of virtual visits within the overall industry.

“With the release of Pulse, Teladoc now has the opportunity to provide an AI-based classification system that identifies patients with chronic conditions that may be showing signs of deterioration and thereby creates opportunities for costly acute-care episodes. This separates them from the traditional model and improves their value proposition,” the analyst noted.

For the full year, Integrated Care revenue is expected to grow 0.4% to 3.9%, with a full-year adjusted EBITDA margin of 15.1% to 16.1%. U.S. integrated care members are forecast to range from 97 million to 100 million by year's end.

Teladoc expects full-year 2026 consolidated revenue between $2.47 billion and $2.59 billion. Adjusted EBITDA is forecast in the range of $266 million to $308 million, reflecting 2% year-over-year growth at the midpoint.

data.title: Roku adds Peacock Premium to channel selection
data.description:
• Roku (ROKU) has added NBCUniversal’s (CMCSA) Peacock Premium Plus to the Roku Channel.
  • Customers can now subscribe directly to the Peacock Premium channel through their Roku (ROKU) account for $16.99 per month or $169.99 per year.
  • With the addition of Peacock Premium, viewers can now watch live sports, including Major League Baseball, the FIFA World Cup in Spanish, and NFL Sunday Night Football.

data.title: Microsoft likely to benefit from deeper Anthropic ties, HSBC says
data.description:
Microsoft (MSFT) is set to report fiscal third-quarter results on April 29, and investment firm HSBC said its deeper ties with Anthropic (ANTHRO) could be the push the Windows maker needs.

“We estimate that Anthropic’s annual revenue run rate surged to [$30B] in April 2026 from [$9B+] in December 2025,” HSBC analyst Stephen Bersey wrote in a note to clients. “While Microsoft agreed to invest [$5B] in Anthropic in November 2025, Anthropic agreed to rent $30B of compute from Microsoft as part of the deal. We see the potential for more such deals to be signed in the near future, further bolstering Azure’s revenue outlook. We believe that OpenAI accounted for c45% of Microsoft’s remaining performance obligation of [$631B] as of 2QFY26 and see Anthropic as a second major source of orders, further diversifying Microsoft’s customer concentration exposure to OpenAI.”

Bersey has a Buy rating on Microsoft and upped his price target on the stock to $593 from $588.

Regarding the upcoming earnings report, Bersey said Azure is still the main growth driver, with expectations that revenue will grow at a 33.6% compound annual rate until 2030. 

“We think that Microsoft is well positioned to leverage AI across its enterprise exposure. Microsoft is a major provider of cloud infrastructure for foundation model providers such as OpenAI and Anthropic and we see continued shortages of AI compute continuing to drive new large customers to Azure,” Bersey added. “Moreover, Microsoft has indirect financial exposure to the foundation model provider markets through its 27% stake and revenue sharing agreement with OpenAI.”

data.title: AbbVie Q1 Preview: Skyrizi, Rinvoq expected to drive growth recovery
data.description:
AbbVie (ABBV) is scheduled to report Q1 earnings on April 29, before market open, with analysts expecting growth recovery both in terms of profits and dividends.

EPS is seen growing 8.5% Y/Y to $2.67, while revenue is projected at $14.72B, translating to a 10.3% Y/Y growth. The revenue figure matches the company’s own guidance.

Seeking Alpha Envision Research noted that growth in recent years was muted due to a combination of sales pressure from the Humira franchise following the patent cliff and also the increased expenses, which it argues have run their course.

Going forward, growth is expected to be driven by Skyrizi and Rinvoq, which analysts believe have more than counterbalanced the decline due to Humira.

“Looking past the next quarter, the longer-term earnings projection for AbbVie indicates a growth trajectory with CAGR in the upper single digits,” it added.

For the full year, management expects total sales growth of 9.5% with net revenues of around $67 billion. The company anticipates an adjusted gross margin above 84% of sales and an adjusted operating margin ratio of approximately 48.5%.

AbbVie expects its key growth drivers to remain robust, with Skyrizi projected to generate $21.5 billion in revenue, Rinvoq $10.1 billion, and Humira $2.9 billion. In neuroscience, global sales are forecast to reach $12.5 billion, led by Vraylar at $4 billion, Botox Therapeutic at $4.1 billion, and Vyalev at $1 billion. Meanwhile, oncology revenue is expected to total $6.5 billion, while the aesthetics segment is projected to deliver $5 billion in sales.

On the dividend front, the analyst noted that growth appears to be regaining momentum, with the latest annual increase of 5.48% suggesting stabilization following a recent slowdown.

Over the last 2 years, ABBV has beaten EPS estimates 75% of the time and has topped revenue estimates 100% of the time, setting up expectations for another better-than-expected quarter.

data.title: Corning falls after Q2 sales outlook misses estimates
data.description:
Shares of Corning (GLW) fell about 5% on Tuesday after the second quarter's sales guidance of $4.6B came in below analysts' estimate of $4.67B.

However, the first-quarter results beat estimates.

Q1 Metrics

Core sales jumped 18% year-over-year to about $4.35B. Core EPS surged around 30% year-over-year to $0.70. Both top and bottom line numbers beat analysts' expectations.

"Additionally, we finalized two more hyperscaler deals similar in size and duration to our recently announced multiyear, up-to-$6 billion agreement with Meta. In total, we have powerful momentum across our Market-Access Platforms," said Chairman, President and CEO Wendell Weeks. "Based on strong demand for our innovations, we plan to upgrade and extend our Springboard plan through 2030 at our May 6 investor event. We will introduce our new Photonics Market-Access Platform and explain the underlying technical and demand trends driving our Gen AI product development."

First quarter net sales from the Optical Communications unit soared 36% year-over-year to around $1.85B.

The company created a Glass Innovations segment, combining its former Display and Specialty Materials segments. Sales from this unit grew 1% year-over-year to $1.42B.

Net sales from its Automotive unit fell 1% year-over-year to $437M. Meanwhile, net sales from the Solar unit surged 80% year-over-year to $370M.

Outlook

Corning said that for the second quarter, management expects to grow core sales about 14% year over year to about $4.6B, versus a consensus estimate of $4.67B.

The company said the second-quarter guidance includes an extended maintenance shutdown at Corning’s solar wafer facility, including the transition to a permanent power system while the company repairs, upgrades, and modifies production equipment to increase throughput in future quarters. This will cause an additional $30M of expense in the second quarter versus the first quarter and is included in guidance, the company added.

Corning expects core EPS to grow about 25% year over year to a range of $0.73 to $0.77 (midpoint at $0.75) versus a consensus estimate of $0.75.

Wall Street Analysts

J.P. Morgan kept its Neutral rating on Corning's stock.

Analysts led by Samik Chatterjee said that Corning’s results were modestly better than expected on both revenue and earnings, with Optical (+36% year-over-year) the primary driver of upside relative to J.P. Morgan's estimates (+5%) and consensus (+6%) on revenue, and even as aggregate company earnings tracked more in line with expectations.

"For 2Q26, management’s guidance was modestly softer relative to our estimates and consensus, with the earnings outlook partly weighed by an extended maintenance shutdown of Corning’s solar wafer facility to increase throughput in future quarters as well as limited growth drivers in non-optical segments which still contribute a majority of revenue and earnings. We expect the results and hyperscaler announcements to support the bull case on the Optical segment, although the magnitude of upsides at the total company level are likely to fall short of elevated investor expectations (embedded in the shares that are up 92% YTD, vs. 5% for the SPX) given lower momentum in non-Optical segments," said Chatterjee and his team.

Update: The story was updated with J.P. Morgan analysts' note and stock price movement.
